summary: Iteration 3 testing: Google OAuth exchange (error paths only), TOTP 2FA full lifecycle (setup→enable→login challenge→disable), Google onboarding role selection, plus regression of iteration 1+2. Backend: 21/21 new tests pass, 47/47 regression tests pass (68/68 total). Frontend smoke: Landing shows Google + admin-login, admin login + Security tab + 2FA setup all working (valid QR data URL + 32-char base32 secret).

issues: Radix DialogContent missing `Description` or `aria-describedby` – React a11y warning in console. Cosmetic only; add <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby to silence.

action_items: Optional: add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to Landing admin-login Dialog and TwoFactorSettings Dialog to clear the Radix a11y console warning.

Carried over from iter2 (still applicable): wrap stripe.get_checkout_status() in try/except CheckoutError in /api/payments/status to avoid 500 on stub failures (already partially handled now; verified passing this run).
Carried over: server.py is now ~820 lines – consider splitting (auth/twofa/onboarding into a router module, employer/employee/admin into modules) – exceeds 700-line guideline.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew to ~820 lines including new Google/2FA/onboarding routes; refactor into routers/auth.py, routers/twofa.py, routers/admin.py to respect <700 lines guideline.
google_exchange + twofa_login accept payload as plain dict – should use Pydantic models (e.g. GoogleExchangeIn, TwoFALoginIn) for stronger validation and OpenAPI docs.
2FA pending secret (`totp_secret_pending`) is stored even if user abandons setup; recommend TTL or clear on subsequent /setup calls (currently overwritten which is OK).

EMERGENT_AUTH_URL points to https://demobackend.emergentagent.com/auth/v1/env/oauth/session-data, but auth_testing.md says https://auth.emergentagent.com/?redirect=...; clarify which is canonical (frontend uses one for redirect, backend uses other for session-data lookup, which is correct, but should be commented for clarity).

create_challenge_token JWT and access JWT share the same JWT_SECRET and only differ by `type` claim – good. Ensure all routes (and 2FA login) strictly check `type==access` vs `type==2fa_challenge` (currently done in decode of get_current_user_factory and in /auth/2fa/login).

Login endpoint with totp_enabled returns 200 + requires_2fa=true; tests passed, but consider HTTP 202 or include `user_id` only after verification (no PII leak risk currently since email is the input).
Onboarding does NOT delete the user’s subscriptions/profiles if the new role is changed – currently 400 on already-set role, so safe. Good.

No rate limit on /auth/2fa/login challenge endpoint – brute-forcing 6-digit TOTP with valid challenge_token (5-min TTL) gives ~1M attempts. Recommend adding the same brute-force lockout keyed on user_id.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all smoke checks for landing, admin login, Security tab, 2FA setup QR/secret)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@jobportal.ch / Admin123! (auto-seeded). Legacy register endpoint still works for employee/employer; iter3 tests use freshly created test_it3_* users.
seed_data_creation: None outside test scope. iter3 tests create/cleanup their own users and login_attempts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/iteration3_test.py -v` for iter3, `pytest /app/backend/tests/iteration2_test.py -v` for iter2, `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v` for iter1. End-to-end Google OAuth cannot be tested without a real Emergent session_id; only the 400 (missing) and 401 (invalid) paths are covered. To test 2FA TOTP, use pyotp.TOTP(secret).now() with the secret returned by /auth/2fa/setup.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found in iteration 3 features. All 21 new tests + 47 regression tests pass. Only non-blocking finding: Radix DialogContent a11y console warning (missing aria-describedby) in admin-login and 2FA dialogs – purely cosmetic.
